heading: Withholding of tax on foreign corporations
label: 1442 Withholding of tax on foreign corporations
notes: References in TextThe date of the enactment of this paragraph, referred to in subsec. (c)(2)(B), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 108–357Oct. 22, 2004

(a). , substituted “the reference in section 1441(c)(10)” for “and the reference in section 1441(c)(10)” and inserted before period at end “, and the references in section 1441(c)(12) to sections 871(a) and 871(k) shall be treated as referring to sections 881(a) and 881(e) (except that for purposes of applying subparagraph (A) of section 1441(c)(12), as so modified, clause (ii) of section 881(e)(1)(B) shall not apply to any dividend unless the regulated investment company knows that such dividend is a dividend referred to in such clause)”.Pub. L. 100–6471986—Subsec. (a). , substituted “871(h)” for “871(h)(2)”, “881(c)” for “881(c)(2)”, and “1441(c)(9)” for “1449(c)(9)”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1810(d)(3)(E)Subsec. (c). , amended subsec. L. 98–369, § 474(r)(29)(I), struck out “and” after “section 881(a)(4),” and inserted “, and the references in section 1449(c)(9) to sections 871(h)(2) and 871(h)(3) shall be treated as referring to sections 881(c)(2) and 881(c)(3)”.Pub. L. 98–369, § 130(b)1976—Subsec. (b). struck out “or his delegate” after “Secretary” in two places.Pub. L. 94–4551972—Subsec. (c). added subsec. (c).Pub. L. 92–6061971—Subsec. (a). provided that reference in section 1441(c)(8) to section 871(a)(1)(C) shall be treated as referring to section 881(a)(3).Pub. L. 92–1781966— limited the withholding of tax at the 30 percent rate to items of fixed or determinable United States source income not effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business in the United States and authorized the granting of an exemption from the withholding requirement in the case of a foreign corporation engaged in trade or business within the United States if the Secretary or his delegate determines that the withholding imposes an undue administrative burden and that the collection of the tax will not be jeopardized by the exemption.Pub. L. 89–809

Withholding of Tax on Nonresident Aliens and Foreign CorporationsFor provisions relating to withholding of tax on nonresident aliens and foreign corporations, see , , , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 97–248, title III, § 34296 Stat. 635section 1441 of this titleSept. 3, 1982

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: In the case of foreign corporations subject to taxation under this subtitle, there shall be deducted and withheld at the source in the same manner and on the same items of income as is provided in section 1441 a tax equal to 30 percent thereof. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the references in section 1441(b) to sections 871(a)(1)(C) and (D) shall be treated as referring to sections 881(a)(3) and (4), the reference in section 1441(c)(1) to section 871(b)(2) shall be treated as referring to section 842 or section 882(a)(2), as the case may be, the reference in section 1441(c)(5) to section 871(a)(1)(D) shall be treated as referring to section 881(a)(4), the reference in section 1441(c)(8) to section 871(a)(1)(C) shall be treated as referring to section 881(a)(3), the references in section 1441(c)(9) to sections 871(h) and 871(h)(3) or (4) shall be treated as referring to sections 881(c) and 881(c)(3) or (4), the reference in section 1441(c)(10) to section 871(i)(2) shall be treated as referring to section 881(d), and the references in section 1441(c)(12) to sections 871(a) and 871(k) shall be treated as referring to sections 881(a) and 881(e) (except that for purposes of applying subparagraph (A) of section 1441(c)(12), as so modified, clause (ii) of section 881(e)(1)(B) shall not apply to any dividend unless the regulated investment company knows that such dividend is a dividend referred to in such clause).

children.heading: Exemption
children.label: b Exemption
children.text: Subject to such terms and conditions as may be provided by regulations prescribed by the Secretary, subsection (a) shall not apply in the case of a foreign corporation engaged in trade or business within the United States if the Secretary determines that the requirements of subsection (a) impose an undue administrative burden and that the collection of the tax imposed by section 881 on such corporation will not be jeopardized by the exemption.

children.heading: Exception for certain possessions corporations
children.label: c Exception for certain possessions corporations

children.heading: Guam, American Samoa, the Northern Mariana Islands, and the Virgin Islands
children.label: 1 Guam, American Samoa, the Northern Mariana Islands, and the Virgin Islands
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “foreign corporation” does not include a corporation created or organized in Guam, American Samoa, the Northern Mariana Islands, or the Virgin Islands or under the law of any such possession if the requirements of subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C) of section 881(b)(1) are met with respect to such corporation.

children.label: i
children.text: created or organized in, or under the law of, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to which the requirements of subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C) of section 881(b)(1) are met for the taxable year,

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: B Applicability
children.text: If, on or after the date of the enactment of this paragraph, an increase in the rate of the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico’s withholding tax which is generally applicable to dividends paid to United States corporations not engaged in a trade or business in the Commonwealth to a rate greater than 10 percent takes effect, this paragraph shall not apply to dividends received on or after the effective date of the increase.
